popularStory.headline: Biography
popularStory.description:
From http://www.historyofparliamentonline.org/volume/1386-1421/member/russell-sir-john-1405 

The Russells of Worcestershire were among the most notable figures to bear the name. They were established …

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Russell於1340年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾斯特倫善姆時，他的父親Robert Russell26歲，母親Katherine Vampage24歲。他於1354年與Agnes De La Planche在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾斯特倫善姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1405年1月31日在英國英格蘭素福爾可列黑林加姆享年65歲，埋葬在英國武斯特斯爾斯特倫善姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Russell于1340年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾斯特倫善姆时，他的父亲Robert Russell26岁，母亲Katherine Vampage24岁。他于1354年与Agnes De La Planche在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾斯特倫善姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1405年1月31日在英国英格兰素福爾可列黑林加姆享年65岁，埋葬在英国武斯特斯爾斯特倫善姆。

nameMeanings.name: Russell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: of Norman origin, from Old French and Anglo-Norman French r(o)ussel, a diminutive of Old French rous(e) ‘red, reddish’, used either as a nickname for someone with red hair or a ruddy complexion, or as a personal name. Compare Rouse . This Norman name has been established in Ireland since the 12th century. It has been reinforced in Britain and Ireland by Huguenot bearers of the name Roussel, of the same Old French origin.

English: habitational name from any of several places called Rushall (Norfolk, Staffordshire, Wiltshire) or possibly sometimes from Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent). Rushall in Staffordshire derives from Old English rysc ‘rush, rushes’ + halh ‘nook, corner of land’. Rushall in Norfolk derives from an uncertain first element + Old English halh. Rushall in Wiltshire derives from an Old English personal name Rust (genitive Rustes) + halh. Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent) probably derives from Old English rust ‘rust, rust color’ + wella ‘well, spring, stream’, but with a change in the final element due to influence from Middle English, Old English hall ‘hall, residence’, perhaps referring to a nearby building.
Americanized form of German Rüssel, from a pet form of any of various personal names formed with the element hrōd ‘fame, renown’.

variants.name: Russler
meanings: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of rüsseln ‘to shallow plow’.

variants.name: Rouse
meanings: English: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Middle English, Anglo-Norman French rous(e) ‘red(-haired)’ (from Latin russ(e)us).
Walloon and northern French (Rousé): variant of Rosé (see Rose ).

variants.name: Russel
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Russell  .
German: from a pet form of Russ, see Russ 2.
northwest German (Rüssel): probably a variant of Rossel .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When John Russell was born in 1340, in Strensham, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Russell, was 26 and his mother, Katherine Vampage, was 24. He married Agnes De La Planche in 1354, in Strensham, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 31 January 1405, in Letheringham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65, and was buried in Strensham, Worcestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Russell est né en 1340, à Strensham, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Russell, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Katherine Vampage, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Agnes De La Planche en 1354, à Strensham, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 31 janvier 1405, à Letheringham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Strensham, Worcestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Russell 1340 in Strensham, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Russell 26 und seine Mutter Katherine Vampage 24. Er hat 1354 in Strensham, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom Agnes De La Planche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 31. Januar 1405 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Letheringham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Strensham, Worcestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Russell è nato il nel 1340 nel Strensham, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert Russell, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Katherine Vampage, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Agnes De La Planche nel 1354, nel Strensham, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 31 gennaio 1405, nel Letheringham, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Strensham, Worcestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John Russellが1340年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアストレンシャムで生まれた時，父Robert Russellは26歳，母Katherine Vampageは24歳であった。1354年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアストレンシャムでAgnes De La Plancheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1405年1月31日にイギリスイギリススフォルクレスエリンガムで65歳で死去し，イギリスウオルセステルシュアイアストレンシャムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Russell(이)가 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 스트렌샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Russell(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Vampage(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1354년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 스트렌샤에서 Agnes De La Planche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1405년 1월 31일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 레더잉함에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워스스터실르 스트렌샤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Russell nasceu em 1340, em Strensham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Russell, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Vampage, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes De La Planche em 1354, em Strensham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 31 de janeiro de 1405, em Letheringham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Strensham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Russell родился в 1340 г. в местности стреншам, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Russell было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Vampage, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes De La Planche в 1354 г. в местности стреншам, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 31 января 1405 г. в местности летирингхам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности стреншам, ворсестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Russell nació en 1340, en Strensham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Russell, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Katherine Vampage, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Agnes De La Planche en 1354, en Strensham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 31 de enero de 1405, en Letheringham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Strensham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.
